journal.name: Journal
journal.desc:
This is Joe's Journal.

Joe keeps track of everything here.

press_card.name: Press Card
press_card.desc:
Joe Constantino,
Reporter for The Times.

Valid until: August 1971

Without this, the guards at Congress
won't let him in.

wallet.name: Wallet
wallet.desc:
A typical Filipino wallet.

Contains a few hundred pesos, identific
-ation cards, and pictures of family.

flashlight.name: Flashlight
flashlight.desc: Can be used to shine light on dark areas.

note.name: Ric's note
note.desc:
President Ferdinand Marcos' State of
the Nation Address will happen on the
Congress Building, located along:

Padre Burgos Drive
Ermita
1000 Manila

estimate_count.name: Number of demonstrators
estimate_count.desc:
If you’d ask me, I’d say there’s around
20,000 of them.

no_guns.name: No guns
no_guns.desc:
Being with the demonstrators did not
make me feel uneasy.

None of them carried guns.

podium_nusp.name: NUSP President
podium_nusp.desc:
He’s Edgar Jopson, the head of the
National Union of Students of
the Philippines (NUSP).

effigies_general.name: Creative effigies
effigies_general.desc:
The student activists are at it again with
their effigies.

What could the coffin represent?

demo_objective.name: Rally demands
demo_objective.desc:
The NUSP is here to appeal for a nonpartisan
Constitutional Convention from the president
and to ask him to refrain from running for a third term.

effigies_meaning.name: Effigy symbolism
effigies_meaning.desc:
The cardboard coffin represents the death of democracy.
The cardboard crocodile symbolizes the cronies and
greed of congressmen for money.

invited_orgs.name: Allied youth groups
invited_orgs.desc:
Other organizations present include KM
(Kabataan Makabayan) and the SDK
(Samahang Demokratiko ng Kabataan).

cops_reputation.name: Police street rep
cops_reputation.desc:
Two words – corrupt and brutal.
They serve only the interests of the rich and powerful.

e1.title: SONA Location
e1.description:
IT WAS FIVE MINUTES PAST FIVE in the afternoon,
by the clock on the Maharnilad tower, when I
arrived at [?].

e1.title: Session Hall Doors
e1.description: The doors of the session hall were [?].

e1.title: Crowd Estimate
e1.description:
There were, according to conservative estimates,
 at least [?] of them, perhaps even 50,000.

e1.title: Speaker
e1.description:
There were two mikes, taped together; … They were
in the hands of [?] Jopson of the National Union of Students of the Philippines,
the group that had organized the rally and secured the permit for it.

e1.title: Demonstrators' guns
e1.description:
Among the demonstrators it was possible to feel at
ease. [?] of them carried guns, they didn’t stand on ceremony,
 and there was no need for the aura of privilege that a press badge automatically
confers on its wearer.

e1.title: Chief Objective
e1.description: Its chief objective was to demand [?].

e1.title: Effigies
e1.description:
WHERE THE DEMONSTRATION LEADERS STOOD, emblems of the
enemy were prominently displayed: a cardboard [?] representing the
death of democracy at the hands of the goonstabulary in the last elections.

e1.title: Weapons in the Chaos
e1.description:
The demonstrators who got as far as the sidewalk that led
to the Muni golf links started to pick up pebbles and rocks with
which they pelted the police. Very soon, placards had
turned into missiles, and the sound of broken glass punctuated the yelling [?] were flying, too. The effigy was down on the ground, still burning.

e1.title: Perception of cops
e1.description: … the cops are generally believed to be [?]

e1.title: Demonstartors' vehicle
e1.description:
The demonstrators had hired a [?] in which rode some of
their leaders. It had two loudspeakers on its roof,
was surrounded by students, and inched its way
forward and backward throughout the melee.

e1.title: Cops actions
e1.description:
When the cops were through [?] the jeepney’s passengers,
they backed away.

e1.title: Injured People Count
e1.description:
More than [?] demonstrators with bloody heads
and broken wrists had been or were being treated along with
three or four policemen hit by rocks.

joe_intro2.text: I need to make sure that I didn’t forget anything before I go.

joe_target_board.text:
The bulletin board. Everyone's kept up to date with everything
that's happening, thanks to that.

joe_newspapers.text: They keep copies of today's edition in the rack.

joe_joe.text: I don't think anything useful will come out of clicking me.

joe_light1.text: There's nothing wrong with the light fixtures, as far as I'm concerned.

joe_light2.text: They are your standard, run-of-the-mill lights.

joe_tut2.text:
You might want to CLICK on the desk and take everything that's
important: my press card, journal, pen, and the like.

joe_desk.text: Let's see... I'll take my press card, journal, pen, and wallet.

joe_item_stapler.text: I don't really need to take a stapler with me.

joe_item_wallet.text: I inherited this from my father. It surely stood the test of time.

joe_item_reports.text: I need to bring this to the archives. On second thought, maybe later.

joe_item_typewriter.text: An Olympia typewriter. This is where I write all my draft reports.

joe_item_mail.text: I don't have anything new at the moment.

joe_item_pencils.text: Of course, I couldn't write without a pen.

joe_item_pencils_empty.text: All that's left is a couple of pencils.

joe_item_papers.text: A pile of empty papers waiting for something to be written.

joe_item_journal.text: Got it. I can't leave without my journal.

joe_item_press_card.text: Yes, that’s me. Joe Constantino, reporting for The Times.

joe_item_note.text: The note says the place of my assignment.

joe_item_flashlight.text: This flashlight will certainly be useful in the future.

joe_return.text: On second thought, I might have forgotten something.

tut_hovercursor.text:
Hover over the items in your screen to perform an action on them.
The cursor changes based on its location.

tut_rightclickreturn.text:
You can return to the previous area by clicking on the empty spots in the screen,
right clicking anywhere, or on areas with a zoom-out icon as the cursor.

joe_choice1.text: Manong, take me to Luneta Park.

joe_choice2.text: Manong, take me to the Congress building.

driver_choice2.text:
I can only give you a ride until Luneta. The entire stretch of the road is
occupied by demonstrators.

joe_choice3_2.text: On second thought, I don’t need to go home right now.

driver_choice3_5.text: Where are you headed to, then?

driver_pay1.text: We’re here, sir. Pay up, please.

joe_pay1.text: Let me just get my wallet.

joe_pay2b.text: It looks like I forgot my wallet.

joe_text2.text: It’s already five minutes past five.

joe_target_speaker.text: The president’s already delivering his SONA message.

joe_target_estimate_count.text: If you’d ask me, I’d say there’s around 20,000 of them.

joe_target_no_guns.text: None of the demonstrators carried guns.

joe_target_effigies.text: The student activists are at it again with their effigies.

joe_target_person_speaking1.text:
He’s Edgar Jopson, the head of the National Union of Students
of the Philippines (NUSP).

joe_target_person_speaking2.text: They’re the group that organized and secured a permit for the rally.

joe_target_police.text:
They’re uniformed policemen, swinging their long rattan sticks, along
with members of the riot squad.

joe_intro.text: I'm Joe Constantino, from The Times. You need my press card?

police_mac_wp_1.text: Unfortunately, Mr. Constantino, the session hall is already closed.

police_mac_wp_2.text:
Letting you in means letting everyone in, including those photographers who
arrived late. We cannot let you and everyone else disturb the order inside.

joe_np_1.text:
Uh. It’s not in my pockets. I seem to have forgotten my press card at my desk back
in the office.

police_mac_np_1.text: You must be one of the militants pretending to be with the press.

police_mac_np_2.text: Please leave now if you don’t want any trouble.

joe_intro.text: Maybe I should speak with one of the demonstrators instead.

joe_talk1.text: I am with The Times, and I would like your view of this demonstration.

joe_talk2.text: I want your side recorded accurately, so let us keep this direct.

dan_talk1.text: Yeah, all right. Ask what you need.

dan_talk2.text: Ask straight, and I'll answer straight.

joe_outro_result.text: That wraps my questions. I've got enough to write this clean.

dan_outro_result.text: That's all, then? All right. Just make sure the paper carries what we actually said.

joe_q1_text1.text: You’re a member of what organization?

joe_q1_text2.text: I need the exact name for my notes.

dan_q1_text2.text: National Union of Students of the Philippines.

dan_q1_doubt_talk1.text: Of course. NUSP's why I'm here.

joe_q1_fact_talk1.text: NUSP, understood. I have seen your posters around the university.

dan_q1_fact_talk1.text: We're trying to reach as many people as possible, sir.

joe_q1_accuse_talk1.text: Let me be direct: are you linked to any underground cadre?

dan_q1_accuse_talk2.text: That's absurd, Sir Joe. You can't brand every student activist a communist.

joe_q2_talk1.text: What’s the objective of this demonstration?

joe_q2_talk2.text: What are you asking the president to do, specifically?

dan_q2_talk1.text:
We are here to appeal for a nonpartisan Constitutional Convention from
the president and to ask him to refrain from running for a third term.

dan_q2_talk2.text: That's the demand, and we've been clear about it all afternoon.

joe_q2_doubt_talk1.text: That is the public line. Is there anything beyond it?

dan_q2_doubt_talk1.text: Not true, Sir Joe. We have said exactly what we came here for.

joe_q2_fact_talk1.text: A nonpartisan convention and term limits. That is clear enough.

joe_q2_accuse_talk1.text: Critics say this rally is built to inflame rather than negotiate.

dan_q2_accuse_talk1.text: Our demands are printed on every banner here, Sir Joe. There is nothing hidden.

joe_q3_talk1.text: For you, what does the effigies symbolize?

joe_q3_talk2.text: Start with the coffin. People can read that in many ways.

dan_q3_talk1.text: The cardboard coffin represents the death of democracy.

dan_q3_talk2.text:
While the cardboard crocodile symbolizes his cronies and the greed of
congressmen for money.

joe_q3_doubt_talk1.text: That coffin image can be read as a threat by some.

dan_q3_doubt_talk1.text: It's papier-mâché, Sir Joe. A symbol of democracy's death — nothing more.

joe_q3_accuse_talk1.text: That coffin imagery can be taken as incitement, whether intended or not.

dan_q3_accuse_talk1.text: The coffin stands for dead democracy, not a person. Surely a journalist can read a symbol.

joe_q4_talk2.text: Who else is coordinating with your group on the ground?

dan_q4_talk1.text:
I’m not really that familiar with everyone here, but there’s KM
(Kabataan Makabayan) and the SDK (Samahang Demokratiko ng Kabataan).

dan_q4_talk2.text: Big crowd, many banners. I only know some of them by name.

joe_q4_doubt_talk1.text: In a coalition this visible, someone keeps track of who is who.

dan_q4_doubt_talk1.text: Sharing a rallyground doesn't mean I know every organization here by heart.

joe_q4_accuse_talk1.text: You’re withholding names, and that raises questions.

dan_q4_accuse_talk1.text: Attending the same rally does not make me their spokesman, Sir Joe.

joe_q4_fact_talk1.text: Youth groups and student unions. It is notable that you are working together.

dan_q4_fact_talk1.text: Unity is our only strength against the administration.

joe_q3_fact_talk1.text: A coffin for democracy and a crocodile for Congress. Hard to miss the point.

dan_q3_fact_talk1.text: Exactly. We want people to see what this government is doing to the country.

joe_q5_fact_talk1.text: From where you stand, you see the police as a threat.

dan_q5_fact_talk1.text: They're usually the ones who start it.

joe_q5_talk1.text: What is your view of the police here to maintain order?

joe_q5_talk2.text: From your position, how are they handling the crowd?

dan_q5_talk1.text:
Two words – corrupt and brutal. They are here to serve only the interests of
the rich and powerful.

dan_q5_talk2.text: They don't protect us. They pressure us.

joe_q5_doubt_talk1.text: Police spokesmen say they are here to contain, not provoke.

dan_q5_doubt_talk1.text: Sir Joe, if you’re here to defend the police, please just go away.

joe_q5_accuse_talk1.text: If those claims are overstated, this line could react to the wrong signal.

dan_q5_accuse_talk1.text:
We are fighting for our rights peacefully, and here you are, throwing
far-fetched accusations.

joe_q6_talk1.text: How many people are here, in your estimate? It is difficult to tell from this distance.

joe_q6_talk2.text: Give me your best estimate, not a slogan.

dan_q6_talk1.text: I'd bet there are at least 50,000 students and workers here today.

dan_q6_talk2.text: Every street near Congress is packed.

joe_q6_doubt_talk1.text: Fifty thousand? That seems high. I have seen smaller crowds look larger than this.

dan_q6_doubt_talk1.text: Count them yourself, Sir Joe. The crowd runs from here all the way to Luneta.

joe_q6_accuse_clue1.text: This estimate looks inflated for effect.

dan_q6_accuse_clue1.text: Look at the streets! How can you say we're a minority?

joe_q6_accuse_success1.text: My own estimates place the crowd much closer to 20,000.

dan_q6_accuse_success1.text: Fine... maybe my enthusiasm got the better of me. 20,000 is still a force to be reckoned with.

joe_q6_fact_talk1.text: It is a large turnout, whichever estimate we use.

dan_q6_fact_talk1.text: We're here to be heard, not to cause trouble.

joe_q7_talk1.text: Who is leading this effort? I heard someone speaking over the PA earlier.

joe_q7_talk2.text: Someone planned this route and timing. Who?

dan_q7_talk1.text: Just some students who care about their country. We don't have 'leaders' in the way you think.

joe_q7_doubt_talk1.text: A rally this coordinated usually has identifiable organizers.

dan_q7_doubt_talk1.text: The people's will is the brain, sir.

joe_q7_accuse_clue1.text: You may be shielding your coordinators from scrutiny.

joe_q7_accuse_success1.text: I recognize Edgar Jopson on that podium. He is NUSP president, is he not?

dan_q7_accuse_success1.text: Yes... Edgar is our president. But he's just one of many voices.

dan_q7_accuse_clue1.text: We are not hiding anyone, Sir Joe. Our leaders stand at that podium for all to see.

joe_q7_fact_talk1.text: So leadership is distributed, not centered on one person.

dan_q7_fact_talk1.text: We're well-organized, Sir Joe. Don't worry about us.

joe_q8_talk1.text: I saw a jeepney with loudspeakers moving with the crowd. Is that yours?

joe_q8_talk2.text: It looked organized, not random transport.

dan_q8_talk1.text: A jeepney? I haven't seen one. We're all on foot.

dan_q8_talk2.text: If there was one, it wasn't under my control.

joe_q8_doubt_talk1.text: It was surrounded by your members. Hard to miss.

dan_q8_doubt_talk1.text: Then it wasn't ours. We came here on foot, same as the others.

joe_q8_accuse_clue1.text: That jeep moved like a command post, not simple transport.

joe_accuse_backdown.text: No, I can't make that stick. Let's move on.

dan_q8_accuse_clue1.text: You're seeing conspiracies where there are only students.

joe_q8_fact_talk1.text: A mobile PA setup. Practical for a crowd this size.

dan_q8_fact_talk1.text: It helps keep everyone's spirits up.

joe_q9_talk1.text: So this is a peaceful demonstration? No weapons?

joe_q9_talk2.text: Not even improvised ones hidden in bags?

dan_q9_talk1.text: None at all. We're here to protest, not to fight.

dan_q9_talk2.text: People brought placards, food, and water. That's it.

joe_q9_fact_talk1.text: Peaceful intent noted. I hope all sides hold to it.

dan_q9_fact_talk1.text: We can only hope, Sir Joe.

joe_q9_doubt_talk1.text: Are you sure no one here is carrying anything? A crowd this size is hard to control.

dan_q9_doubt_talk1.text: I assure you, Sir Joe. We left our weapons at home because we have none to bring.

joe_q9_accuse_talk1.text: Witnesses insist there are concealed weapons in the crowd.

dan_q9_accuse_talk1.text: That is a very serious accusation, Sir Joe. Search every one of us if you want.

joe_q1_fact_talk2.text: All right, NUSP noted in my draft.

joe_q1_doubt_talk2.text: I need a precise answer, not a slogan.

joe_q1_accuse_talk2.text: If not, give me names and details I can verify in print.

joe_q2_doubt_talk2.text: Convince me there is no second track.

joe_q2_accuse_talk2.text: If that is false, show me where your actions prove otherwise.

joe_q3_fact_talk2.text: Symbolic protest, then, not direct threat.

joe_q3_doubt_talk2.text: In a tense crowd, symbols can be misread quickly.

joe_q3_accuse_talk2.text: If you keep that image, people may read threat into it.

joe_q4_fact_talk2.text: A broad coalition, even with internal differences.

joe_q4_doubt_talk2.text: You likely know more names than that.

joe_q4_accuse_talk2.text: Right now, it reads like protection of your network.

joe_q5_fact_talk2.text: I will record that as your account.

joe_q5_doubt_talk2.text: I need specific incidents, not only impressions.

joe_q5_accuse_talk2.text: That language can harden both sides before facts are checked.

joe_q6_doubt_talk2.text: I cannot print numbers I cannot defend.

joe_q6_accuse_clue2.text: Those numbers shift too easily for me to trust.

joe_q7_fact_talk2.text: Loose leadership can still coordinate a movement.

joe_q7_doubt_talk2.text: Movements this coordinated rarely run leaderless.

joe_q7_accuse_clue2.text: Name the coordinators, and we can test the claim.

joe_q8_fact_talk2.text: That explains how messages traveled through the line.

joe_q8_doubt_talk2.text: Then who was operating that vehicle?

joe_q8_accuse_clue2.text: Treating that loudspeaker jeep as incidental is not credible.

joe_q9_fact_talk2.text: Good. I would rather report restraint than blood.

joe_q9_doubt_talk2.text: In this chaos, one hidden weapon changes everything.

joe_q9_accuse_talk2.text: If weapons surface, the peaceful claim fails.

dan_q1_fact_talk2.text: Write that we are students, not infiltrators.

dan_q1_doubt_talk2.text: You can ask anyone here and get the same answer.

dan_q2_fact_talk2.text: Con-Con in 1971, and no third term.

dan_q2_doubt_talk2.text: The banners already say exactly that.

dan_q2_accuse_talk2.text: Read the placards first, Sir Joe, before you accuse us of hidden plans.

dan_q3_fact_talk2.text: The symbols criticize power, not people in this crowd.

dan_q3_doubt_talk2.text: If we wanted violence, we would not use cardboard.

dan_q3_accuse_talk2.text: You are twisting protest symbols into a threat.

dan_q4_doubt_talk2.text: This is a rallyground, not a roll call.

dan_q4_accuse_talk2.text: Knowing names does not make me a handler.

dan_q5_fact_talk2.text: Watch their batons and tell me I am wrong.

dan_q5_doubt_talk2.text: Restraint is not what we are seeing from this line.

dan_q5_accuse_talk2.text: Calling abuse by its name is not incitement, Sir Joe.

dan_q6_fact_talk2.text: Numbers aside, this is still a massive turnout.

dan_q6_doubt_talk2.text: The avenue is full from one end to the other.

dan_q6_accuse_clue2.text: You are quibbling over figures to dodge the message.

dan_q6_accuse_success2.text: Fine, print 20,000 then. The demand still stands.

dan_q7_fact_talk2.text: Coordination is collective, not secret command.

dan_q7_doubt_talk2.text: Organization is visible if you actually look around.

dan_q7_accuse_clue2.text: You keep asking for a hidden chief because it fits the story you came with.

dan_q7_accuse_success2.text: Yes, Edgar is prominent, but he does not speak alone.

dan_q8_fact_talk2.text: A sound system helps people hear, not attack.

dan_q8_doubt_talk2.text: Crowds merge and split. Not every jeep belongs to us.

dan_q8_accuse_clue2.text: A loudspeaker jeep does not make a conspiracy.

dan_q9_fact_talk2.text: Our point is to be seen and heard, not armed.

dan_q9_doubt_talk2.text: Search us and you will find leaflets and raincoats.

dan_q9_accuse_talk2.text: Do not turn fear into evidence, Sir Joe.

joe_intro.text: It’s probably seven o’clock. It’s difficult to see anything.

joe_target_jeep.text:
It’s likely that the jeep was rented out by the demonstrators.
It had two loudspeakers on the roof.

joe_target_riot_talk2.text:
The cops, wearing crash helmets and carrying wicker shields, were
striking out and beating up the students surrounding the jeepney.

joe_target_demonstrator_talk1.text:
He’s downed and crumpled to the ground, his legs to his chest and his
arms over his head.

joe_target_demonstrator_talk2.text: Is that blood? He must’ve been beaten up by those cops.

cop2_end.text: Come closer and you’ll get what you’re looking for.

doc_intro1.text: PGH, Mr. Constantino. You were brought here after passing out.

joe_target_demonstrator.text:
He's one of them. I’d estimate there’s around thirty or so demonstrators
with bloody heads and broken wrists.

joe_target_nusp_talk1.text:
I thought they’ve left early. It seems like some members of the NUSP
weren’t able to escape the violence.

joe_target_police_talk1.text: I see maybe three to four policemen, possibly hit by rocks.

joe_target_doctor_talk1.text: She’s probably busy speaking with the other patients.

joe_target_door_talk1.text: I’ll be on my way, thank you.
